completion_promises.criterion_text: src/jarvis/agents/supervisor.py — build_supervisor adds two keyword-only parameters: async_subagents: list[AsyncSubAgent] | None = None, ambient_tool_factory: Callable[[], list[BaseTool]] | None = None.
completion_promises.evidence: Both kwargs declared keyword-only (after the * marker) on build_supervisor with default None. Imports added: Callable from collections.abc and AsyncSubAgent under TYPE_CHECKING. Verified by TestAC001SignatureShape.test_async_subagents_is_keyword_only_with_default_none and test_ambient_tool_factory_is_keyword_only_with_default_none.

completion_promises.criterion_text: When async_subagents=None: no async subagents wired; the supervisor's tool catalogue does not include the five AsyncSubAgentMiddleware operational tools (scenario: Building the supervisor without the async-subagents argument preserves existing behaviour).
completion_promises.evidence: build_supervisor passes subagents=list(async_subagents) if async_subagents else [] to create_deep_agent — an empty list keeps AsyncSubAgentMiddleware out of the middleware stack so its five tools are not injected. Asserted by TestAC002NoAsyncSubagentsByDefault.test_subagents_kwarg_is_empty_list_when_none and test_compiled_graph_omits_async_middleware_tools (the latter walks the realised graph nodes and asserts the intersection with {start,check,update,cancel,list}_async_task[s] is empty).

completion_promises.criterion_text: When async_subagents=[<jarvis-reasoner>]: AsyncSubAgentMiddleware auto-injects start_async_task, check_async_task, update_async_task, cancel_async_task, list_async_tasks into the supervisor's tool catalogue (scenario: Wiring the async subagent injects the five middleware operational tools).
completion_promises.evidence: When async_subagents is non-empty it is forwarded verbatim as subagents=list(async_subagents) to create_deep_agent, which causes deepagents.graph to append AsyncSubAgentMiddleware(async_subagents=…) — the middleware then registers all five operational tools. Asserted by TestAC003AsyncSubagentInjectsMiddlewareTools.test_subagents_kwarg_carries_supplied_async_specs (kwargs check) and test_compiled_graph_exposes_five_middleware_tools (real-graph introspection — asserts ASYNC_MIDDLEWARE_TOOL_NAMES ⊆ wired).

completion_promises.criterion_text: When ambient_tool_factory=None: ambient/learning paths fall back to assemble_tool_list(..., include_frontier=False) — the attended tool list minus escalate_to_frontier (scenario: Not configuring an ambient tool factory falls back to the attended tools without frontier).
completion_promises.evidence: _default_ambient_tool_factory(config, available_capabilities) returns a closure that lazily imports jarvis.tools.assemble_tool_list and calls it with include_frontier=False. The closure is attached to the compiled graph as graph._jarvis_ambient_tool_factory whenever ambient_tool_factory=None. Asserted by TestAC004AmbientFactoryDefault.test_default_factory_is_attached_when_none, test_default_factory_invokes_assemble_tool_list_without_frontier (patches jarvis.tools.assemble_tool_list and asserts kwargs['include_frontier'] is False), and test_default_factory_returns_real_ambient_list_excluding_frontier (end-to-end: invokes the real factory, asserts 'escalate_to_frontier' ∉ names AND the FEAT-J002 9-tool baseline ⊆ names).

completion_promises.criterion_text: When ambient_tool_factory is supplied: the factory's return value is the canonical ambient tool list.
completion_promises.evidence: When the caller supplies ambient_tool_factory, build_supervisor stores the callable verbatim (no wrapping) on graph._jarvis_ambient_tool_factory. Asserted by TestAC005AmbientFactorySupplied.test_supplied_factory_is_attached_verbatim (asserts attached is my_factory — identity, not equality), test_supplied_factory_return_value_is_canonical (asserts attached() is canonical_list), and test_supplied_factory_does_not_call_assemble_tool_list (asserts neither the supplied factory nor the assemble_tool_list fallback fires at build time — the factory is invoked lazily by ambient consumers).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Phase 1 + FEAT-J002 call sites that supply only tools= and available_capabilities= still return a valid CompiledStateGraph (backward compat).
completion_promises.evidence: Both new kwargs default to None and only affect behaviour when supplied. Verified by (a) TestAC006BackwardCompatibility.test_phase1_call_site_still_returns_compiled_graph (asserts build_supervisor(config) returns CompiledStateGraph), (b) test_feat_j002_call_site_still_returns_compiled_graph (asserts build_supervisor(config, tools=…, available_capabilities=…) returns CompiledStateGraph), (c) test_feat_j002_call_site_omits_async_middleware_tools (asserts the five middleware tools are absent), AND (d) the entire pre-existing supervisor test suite (52 tests in test_supervisor.py + test_supervisor_no_llm_call.py + test_supervisor_with_tools.py + test_supervisor_lifecycle_wiring.py) continues to pass unchanged.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Signature is documented in docstring; parameter order preserves existing ordering (kwargs-only additions).
completion_promises.evidence: build_supervisor's docstring documents both new kwargs by name with default-resolution semantics, plus the resolved-factory attribute (graph._jarvis_ambient_tool_factory). Parameter declaration order is config, *, tools, available_capabilities, async_subagents, ambient_tool_factory — additions sit AFTER the FEAT-J002 kwargs. Verified by TestAC001SignatureShape.test_phase2_kwargs_still_present, test_kwargs_appear_after_feat_j002_kwargs (asserts idx[available_capabilities] < idx[async_subagents] < idx[ambient_tool_factory]), and test_docstring_documents_new_kwargs.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: uv run ruff check src/jarvis/agents/supervisor.py tests/test_supervisor_extended_signature.py → 'All checks passed!' (zero errors). uv run ruff format --check on both files → '2 files already formatted' (zero diffs).
